key_features: Extends BaseController for consistent response handling
9 main endpoint groups: registration, login, token refresh, logout, password reset, email verification, current user, session management, and account management
patterns_used: HTTP-only cookie management for refresh tokens

patterns_discovered.description: Endpoints organized into logical sections with clear comments separating concerns: Registration, Login, Token Refresh, Logout, Password Reset, Email Verification, Current User, Session Management
Refresh tokens stored in HTTP-only cookies, access tokens in response body. Helper methods setRefreshTokenCookie and clearRefreshTokenCookie manage cookie lifecycle
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents XSS token theft while maintaining token rotation capability

patterns_discovered.description: ForgotPassword and resendVerification endpoints always return success to prevent email enumeration attacks
patterns_discovered.benefit: Improves security posture by preventing user enumeration

patterns_discovered.description: All endpoints use TypeScript interfaces from auth.types for request and response payloads
patterns_discovered.benefit: Compile-time type safety and self-documenting API contracts

patterns_discovered.description: All endpoints return ApiResponse via inherited success() method from BaseController
patterns_discovered.benefit: Standardized API response format across all endpoints

patterns_discovered.description: Uses @Authorized() decorator for protected endpoints, @CurrentUser() for injecting user context
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clean, declarative security rules at endpoint level

patterns_discovered.description: Login and refresh endpoints capture IP address and user agent for session tracking
patterns_discovered.benefit: Enables session management and device tracking

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Refresh token in login response excluded intentionally
gotchas_discovered.description: Login endpoint returns access token in response body but refresh token only in HTTP-only cookie. This is intentional design to prevent refresh token exposure
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Design is correct; frontend should not expect refresh token in response body

gotchas_discovered.description: resetPassword endpoint description mentions it 'revokes all existing sessions', which could force re-login on all devices unexpectedly
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Document this behavior clearly in API documentation and frontend error handling

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Logout requires jti from token payload
gotchas_discovered.description: Logout uses req.tokenPayload?.jti with fallback to empty string, which could fail silently if token structure changes
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add validation to ensure jti is present before logout

gotchas_discovered.description: The diff was truncated, so SessionInfo endpoint implementation details are not fully visible
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Review complete file to verify session listing endpoint

deliverables_completed: POST /auth/login - User authentication with token generation
POST /auth/logout - Session termination with HTTP-only cookie clearing
POST /auth/reset-password - Password reset with token validation

recommendations.recommendation: Add rate limiting to authentication endpoints to prevent brute force attacks, especially on login and password reset endpoints
Ensure input validation middleware is applied to all endpoints, particularly email format and password strength requirements
Verify that error responses don't leak information about user existence (timing attacks, error messages)
Add comprehensive integration tests for token refresh flow, cookie handling, and session lifecycle
Document the implication of 'logout from all devices' behavior and refresh token cookie security settings (SameSite, Secure, HttpOnly flags)
Consider adding endpoint for device/session listing and selective session revocation (logout from specific device)
Add audit logging for authentication events (registration, login, password change, logout) with user context and timestamps
Add null/undefined checks for req.tokenPayload?.jti in logout endpoint to fail-safe rather than silently using empty string
